links.title: PREPING: Building Agent Memory without Tasks

primary_link.title: PREPING: Building Agent Memory without Tasks

papers.summary: Agent memory is typically constructed either offline from curated demonstrations or online from post-deployment interactions. However, regardless of how it is built, an agent faces a cold-start gap when first introduced to a new environment without any task-specific experience available. In this paper, we study pre-task memory construction: whether an agent can build procedural memory before observing any target-environment tasks, using only self-generated synthetic practice. Yet, synthetic interaction alone is insufficient, as without controlling what to practice and what to store, synthetic tasks become redundant, infeasible, and ultimately uninformative, and memory further degrades quickly due to unfiltered trajectories. To overcome this, we present Preping, a proposer-guided memory construction framework. At its core is proposer memory, a structured control state that shapes future practice. A Proposer generates synthetic tasks conditioned on this state, a Solver executes them, and a Validator determines which trajectories are eligible for memory insertion while also providing feedback to guide future proposals. Experiments on AppWorld, BFCL v3, and MCP-Universe show that Preping substantially improves over a no-memory baseline and achieves performance competitive with strong playbook-based methods built from offline or online experience, with deployment cost 2.99times lower on AppWorld and 2.23times lower on BFCL v3 than online memory construction. Further analyses reveal that the main benefit does not come from synthetic volume alone, but from proposer-side control over feasibility, redundancy, and coverage, combined with selective memory updates.
papers.summary_zh: Agent 記憶通常以離線方式從精心策劃的示範中構建，或以線上方式從部署後的互動中構建。然而，無論如何構建，當 agent 首次進入一個沒有任何任務特定經驗的新環境時，都會面臨冷啟動缺口。在本文中，我們研究預任務記憶構建：agent 能否在觀察到任何目標環境任務之前，僅使用自我生成的合成練習來構建程序性記憶。然而，僅有合成互動是不夠的，因為若未控制練習什麼和儲存什麼，合成任務將變得冗餘、不可行且最終毫無信息量，且記憶會因未過濾的軌跡而迅速退化。為克服此問題，我們提出 Preping，一個基於提議者引導的記憶構建框架。其核心是提議者記憶，一種結構化的控制狀態，用以塑造未來的練習。提議者根據此狀態生成合成任務，求解者執行它們，驗證者判定哪些軌跡有資格插入記憶，同時提供回饋以引導未來的提議。在 AppWorld、BFCL v3 和 MCP-Universe 上的實驗表明，Preping 相較於無記憶基線有顯著提升，並達到與從離線或線上經驗構建的強 playbook 方法具有競爭力的性能，在 AppWorld 上的部署成本比線上記憶構建低 2.99 倍，在 BFCL v3 上低 2.23 倍。進一步分析揭示，主要收益並非僅來自合成數量，而是來自提議者端對可行性、冗餘性和覆蓋範圍的控制，並結合選擇性的記憶更新。
papers.title: PREPING: Building Agent Memory without Tasks
papers.title_zh: PREPING: 在無任務情況下構建 Agent 記憶

When these checks rely on surface level signals or fail to capture the agent's actual action path, they cannot reliably determine whether the run succeeded. While a natural solution is to calibrate the model's output distribution, for LLMs, this is ill-posed at the combinatorially vast level of free-form language. We address this by building on the insight that in many tasks, these free-form outputs can be interpreted in a semantically meaningful
papers.summary_zh: LLM 解碼通常依賴模型的預測分布來生成輸出。因此，相對於真實生成分布的不對齊會導致實務上次優決策。雖然一個自然的解方是校準模型的輸出分布，但對 LLM 來說，在自由形式語言的組合爆炸層級上這是不適定的。我們透過建立在許多任務中這些自由形式輸出可以在語義有意義上被詮釋的洞見上，來解決此問題...

primary_link.title: MAGE: Multi-Agent Self-Evolution with Co-Evolutionary Knowledge Graphs

radar.summary: Self-evolving language-model agents must decide what to learn next and how to preserve what they have learned across iterations. radar.summary: Tool-using LLM agents must act on untrusted webpages, emails, files, and API outputs while issuing privileged tool calls. primary_link.title: expo: Exploration-prioritized policy optimization via adaptive kl regulation and gaussian curriculum sampling

radar.summary: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has become the standard paradigm for LLM mathematical reasoning, where Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) serves as the mainstream algorithm. radar.summary: While recent self-training approaches have reduced reliance on human-labeled data for aligning LLMs, they still face critical limitations: (i) sensitivity to synthetic data quality, leading to instability and bias amplification in iterative training; (ii) ineffective optimization due to a diminishing gap between positive and negative responses over successive training iterations. primary_link.title: Pseudo-Deliberation in Language Models: When Reasoning Fails to Align Values and Actions

radar.summary: Large language models (LLMs) are often evaluated based on their stated values, yet these do not reliably translate into their actions, a discrepancy termed "value-action gap." radar.summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）經常根據其陳述的價值進行評估，但這些價值並不能可靠地轉化為其行動，這種差距被稱為「價值-行動鴻溝」。在本工作中，我們主張這種鴻溝即使在明確推理下依然存在，揭示出一種更深層的失敗模式，我們稱之為「偽審議」：表面上看似有原則的推理卻缺乏相應的行為對齊。為了系統性地研究此現象，我們引入 VALDI，一個用於衡量陳述

primary_link.title: Sequential Behavioral Watermarking for LLM Agents

radar.summary: LLM-based agents act through sequences of executable decisions, but their trajectories provide little evidence of which agent or policy produced them, making provenance, ownership, and unauthorized reuse difficult to establish from observed behavior alone. radar.summary: The key-value (KV) cache is a major bottleneck in long-context inference, where memory and computation grow with sequence length. Existing KV eviction methods reduce this cost but typically degrade performance relative to full-cache inference. Our key insight is that full-cache attention is not always optimal: in long contexts, irrelevant tokens can dilute attention away from useful evidence, so selective, learnable eviction can improve generation rather than merely approximate the full cache. radar.summary_zh: 鍵值（KV）快取是長上下文推論的主要瓶頸，記憶體和計算量隨序列長度增長。現有的 KV 驅逐方法降低了此成本，但通常會相對於完整快取推論而降低效能。我們的關鍵洞察是，完整快取的注意力並非總是最佳：在長上下文中，不相關的 tokens 會稀釋對有用證據的注意力，因此選擇性、可學習的驅逐可以改善生成結果，而不僅僅是近似完整快取。我們

links.title: WildClawBench: A Benchmark for Real-World, Long-Horizon Agent Evaluation

primary_link.title: WildClawBench: A Benchmark for Real-World, Long-Horizon Agent Evaluation

top5.summary: Large language and vision-language models increasingly power agents that act on a user's behalf through command-line interface (CLI) harnesses. However, most agent benchmarks still rely on synthetic sandboxes, short-horizon tasks, mock-service APIs, and final-answer checks, leaving open whether agents can complete realistic long-horizon work in the runtimes where they are deployed. This work presents WildClawBench, a native-runtime benchmark of 60 human-authored, bilingual, multimodal tasks spanning six thematic categories. Each task averages roughly 8 minutes of wall-clock time and over 20 tool calls, and runs inside a reproducible Docker container hosting an actual CLI agent harness (OpenClaw, Claude Code, Codex, or Hermes Agent) with access to real tools rather than mock services. Grading is hybrid, combining deterministic rule-based checks, environment-state auditing of side effects, and an LLM/VLM judge for semantic verification. Across 19 frontier models, the best, Claude Opus 4.7, reaches only 62.2% overall under OpenClaw, while every other model stays below 60%, and switching harness alone shifts a single model by up to 18 points. These results show that long-horizon, native-runtime agent evaluation remains a far-from-resolved task for current frontier models. We release the tasks, code, and containerized tooling to support reproducible evaluation.
top5.summary_zh: 大型語言與視覺語言模型日益驅動透過命令列介面（CLI）框架代表使用者行動的 agent。然而，大多數 agent 基準仍依賴於合成沙盒、短時程任務、模擬服務 API 和最終答案檢查，未能回答 agent 能否在實際部署的執行環境中完成真實的長時程工作。本工作提出 WildClawBench，一個原生執行環境基準，包含 60 個由人類撰寫、雙語、多模態的任務，涵蓋六大主題類別。每項任務平均需時約 8 分鐘牆鐘時間、超過 20 次工具調用，並在可重現的 Docker 容器中執行，該容器託管實際的 CLI agent 框架（OpenClaw、Claude Code、Codex 或 Hermes Agent），可存取真實工具而非模擬服務。評分方式為混合式，結合確定性規則檢查、對副作用進行環境狀態審計，以及用於語意驗證的 LLM/VLM 評判器。在 19 個前沿模型中，表現最佳者 Claude Opus 4.7 在 OpenClaw 下的總體得分僅達 62.2%，而其他所有模型均低於 60%；僅是切換框架即可使同一模型的表現變動高達 18 個百分點。這些結果表明，長時程、原生執行環境的 agent 評估對當前的前沿模型而言仍是一項遠未解決的任務。我們釋出任務、程式碼和容器化工具，以支援可重現的評估。
